prompt: A photorealistic, wide-angle shot of a massive, seemingly endless highway stretching across a barren desert landscape. The highway is completely gridlocked bumper-to-bumper with heavy, rusted transport trucks. Instead of normal cargo, every single truck is overflowing with crushed, tangled electronic waste—broken keyboards, cracked monitors, and tangled wires spilling over the sides. The mood is heavy and dystopian, under an overcast, oppressive grey sky. Shot on 35mm lens. Absolutely no text, no numbers, no labels, no UI, and no charts.
insight: The world generates enough electronic waste every year to form a continuous line of 40-ton trucks wrapping completely around the Earth's equator.
